summary: Crystal‑clear matrix capture of the March 1990 Cap Centre run, with high‑energy playing and standout Loose Lucy, Playing‑in‑the‑Band/UJB sequence, Good Time Blues and Space‑>Black Peter.
review: The matrix source blends a pristine soundboard feed with vibrant crowd ambience, delivering crisp vocals and clear instrumentation throughout.  The band is in top form – tight, energetic and full of fire – highlighted by a blazing Loose Lucy, an epic Playing‑in‑the‑Band → Uncle John’s Band stretch and superb late‑set jams like Good Time Blues, Dear Mr Fantasy and Space into Black Peter.

ai_rating.rationale: Over ten reviews overwhelmingly rate the show 5 stars and praise both performance and matrix sound quality; only one mixed review tempers the score, yielding a strong overall rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Pristine sound‑board foundation with audience overlay; reviewers repeatedly call it crisp, clear and well‑balanced. One reviewer notes the source isn’t “top‑notch” but still very listenable.

standout_songs: Playing in the Band > Uncle John's Band
Good Time Blues (Never Trust a Woman)
show_quality.setlist_flow: Tight, upbeat first set that builds into an explosive second set; seamless transitions and high crowd participation keep the momentum rolling from start to finish.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Vocals described as powerful but not perfect; guitar work praised for energy, especially on Loose Lucy and the UJB/Playing‑in‑the‑Band jam.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass highlighted repeatedly – “check Phil” on Cold Rain & Snow, solid work on Good Time Blues and Black Peter; noted as healthy during this era.
band_member_comments.Bob: Keyboardist Bobby (Brent) credited with rescuing moments like UJB and adding ideas in Black Peter; Brent praised for peak performance.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (keyboards) singled out as at his peak, delivering sophisticated, jazzy textures throughout the show.
